title: Picanha (Sem crueldade)
rawBody:
- **Source / inspiration URL:** https://braziliankitchenabroad.com/picanha-brazilian-culotte-steaks/ ⚠ REFERENCE (non-vegan / technique) *(Brazilian Kitchen Abroad's recipe is for real-beef culotte steaks — the technique reference for the salt-cure, fat-cap-up sear, and tableside slicing. **Recommended: Juicy Marbles Whole-Cut Loin** (or stacked Thick-Cut Filets). Salt absorption, sear behavior, and rest times all differ vs. real picanha — JM's loin renders fat differently, calibrate during a service test.)*
- **Description:** **Lead protein:** Juicy Marbles Whole-Cut Loin (or stacked Thick-Cut Filets). Heavily salted with rock salt, skewered fat-cap-up (the loin's marbling reads as fat cap), seared/flame-roasted on a churrasqueira rotisserie or ripping-hot plancha, rested, then sliced thin against the grain tableside or à la minute. Served with chimichurri or salsa vinagrete + farofa. The Brazilian centerpiece, plant-based.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $11.80 / serving (1 × JM Loin portion ≈ 6 oz @ ~$32/lb wholesale + sides) · **Menu price:** **$32–38** (steakhouse tier ÷ 0.30)
- **Weight:** 360 g (steak + farofa + vinagrete) · **Calories:** 580 · **Protein:** 36 g · **Time:** 35 min (incl. 30-min salt rest) · **Servings:** 1
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Hard** — open flame or plancha + 30-min salt cure + rest + slice + plated sides; this is your signature dish, plate one at a time.
- **Tags:** (neither — purely cooked-to-order)

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Lead protein:** Juicy Marbles Whole-Cut Loin (default) | Largest plant-meat cut; best presentation | baseline |
| Juicy Marbles Thick-Cut Filet × 2 stacked | Easier portion control | −$2.20 |
| Chunk Foods Steakhouse Cut 6 oz | More savings, slightly less marbling drama | −$5.80 |
| Beyond Steak Tips on skewer (espetinho) | Casual-tier picanha-espetinho variant | −$7.50 (menu repositions to $19–22) |
description: **Lead protein:** Juicy Marbles Whole-Cut Loin (or stacked Thick-Cut Filets). Heavily salted with rock salt, skewered fat-cap-up (the loin's marbling reads as fat cap), seared/flame-roasted on a churrasqueira rotisserie or ripping-hot plancha, rested, then sliced thin against the grain tableside or à la minute. Served with chimichurri or salsa vinagrete + farofa. The Brazilian centerpiece, plant-based.
difficultyNote: open flame or plancha + 30-min salt cure + rest + slice + plated sides; this is your signature dish, plate one at a time.

subs.effect: More savings, slightly less marbling drama

ingredients: 2.5 lbs of Picanha / SRF AMERICAN WAGYU BLACK GRADE CULOTTE
lemon pepper (or freshly ground black pepper to taste)
steps: Preheat the grill to 350F.
In a small bowl, mix the sofrito, the mustard, the dry parsley and a drizzle of olive oil. Set aside.
Cut the meat the same direction as the fibers into 4 steaks.
Season the steaks with salt and the lemon pepper, then rub the sofrito mixture on the steaks.
Add the steaks to the grill, and grill them on all sides to your favorite doneness.
Remove the steaks from the grill, and let them rest for 5 mins before slicing them.
While the steaks are resting make the farofa.
On a stove top melt the butter in a medium skillet over medium heat.
Add the onions to the pan and sauté until the onions are transparent, about 2 minutes.
Add the eggs to the pan, and scramble them gently.
When the eggs are cooked, but still soft, add the cassava and stir.
Remove the pan from the heat, add the cilantro and stir to combine.
Season with salt and pepper.
Slice the steaks across the fibers, and serve with the farofa.

title: Feijoada (Sem crueldade)
rawBody:
- **Source URL:** https://www.thefieryvegetarian.com/brazilian-black-beans-feijoada/ (also Vegan Cocotte: https://vegancocotte.com/vegan-feijoada/)
- **Description:** Slow-stewed black beans built up with smoked tofu, vegan sausage / vegan linguiça, and mushrooms for the meaty smokiness. Served with rice, sliced oranges, sautéed couve (collards), and farofa. The ultimate batch dish — better day 2.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $2.65 / serving · **Menu price:** $14–18 (full plated combo)
- **Weight:** 480 g (beans + rice + sides) · **Calories:** 640 · **Protein:** 28 g · **Time:** 90 min (after overnight soak) · **Servings:** 8
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — quintessential bulk-prep Brazilian dish.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Smoked tofu + mushrooms (default) | Authentic-feeling smoke profile | baseline |
| **Premium faux-meat:** Beyond Sausage (sliced) + Chunk Pulled added in last 15 min | Steakhouse upgrade; menu repositions $18–22 | +$2.40 |
| Field Roast Italian sausage | Closest US foodservice linguiça stand-in | +$1.20 |
description: Slow-stewed black beans built up with smoked tofu, vegan sausage / vegan linguiça, and mushrooms for the meaty smokiness. Served with rice, sliced oranges, sautéed couve (collards), and farofa. The ultimate batch dish — better day 2.

subs.from: **Premium faux-meat:** Beyond Sausage (sliced) + Chunk Pulled added in last 15 min

ingredients: lemon quarters or slices of orange
½ teaspoon black or white ground pepper
2⅔ cups dried black beans (6-8 cups cooked)
1 tablespoon red or white wine vinegar
½ tablespoon Marmite (optional, also known as Vegemite, yeast extract)
steps: 1. Take two of the onions and chop or pulse 6-8 times in a food processor to easily chop. Puree the remaining onion in a food processor or blender and set aside for step 4. 2. Fry the chopped onions in the olive oil over medium-high heat, stirring often, for 10 minutes. Add the 1/2 teaspoon salt at around the 5-6 minute mark when the onions are slightly golden. 3. Add all the spices and seasoning from the garlic to the ground pepper and stir well, toasting for two minutes. 4. Add in the pureed onion and fry for five minutes, stirring often. 5. Now add in the beans water and stock cube. Check the notes for cooking times for all methods. 6. After the beans have finished cooking, bring to the boil and add in the two types of vinegar, soy sauce and marmite (optional). Reduce to a simmer and let cook for five minutes more. 7. Remove the bay leaves. If you'd like to thicken the sauce you can blend it a little, no more than half a cup. Serve!

title: Pão de Queijo (Sem crueldade)
rawTitle: ### Vegan Pão de Queijo (Brazilian Cheese Bread)
rawBody:
- **Source URL:** https://veggiekinsblog.com/2021/02/08/vegan-pao-de-queijo/ (also Salted Plains: https://saltedplains.com/vegan-brazilian-cheese-bread-gluten-free/)
- **Description:** Tapioca-starch dough hydrated with hot plant milk and oil, mixed with mashed potato, vegan parmesan/Violife, and nutritional yeast. Naturally gluten-free; chewy outside, gooey inside. Served warm — a welcome bread or shareable starter at any churrascaria.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $0.40 / piece (~$2.40/6-piece basket) · **Menu price:** $5–7 (basket) / $9–11 (large basket)
- **Weight:** 30 g / piece · **Calories:** 95 / piece · **Protein:** 1.5 g · **Time:** 35 min · **Servings:** ~24 pieces / batch
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Hard** — dough must be mixed and piped fresh per service window for proper puff; doesn't reheat well from cold. Either par-bake & warm in deck oven, or pipe-and-bake during service. Labor-intensive.
- **Tags:** (neither — fresh-bake required)

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Violife / Miyoko's mozz-style melt cheese (default) | Best stretch | baseline |
| Daiya | Avoid — gummy texture per Brazilian-vegan blog reports | n/a |
| Nutritional yeast only (no vegan cheese) | Cleaner ingredient | −$0.10/pc |
description: Tapioca-starch dough hydrated with hot plant milk and oil, mixed with mashed potato, vegan parmesan/Violife, and nutritional yeast. Naturally gluten-free; chewy outside, gooey inside. Served warm — a welcome bread or shareable starter at any churrascaria.
difficultyNote: dough must be mixed and piped fresh per service window for proper puff; doesn't reheat well from cold. Either par-bake & warm in deck oven, or pipe-and-bake during service. Labor-intensive.

subs.effect: Avoid — gummy texture per Brazilian-vegan blog reports

ingredients: 1/4 cup unsweetened plant milk (I used oat milk)
2 cups sour manioc flour or tapioca starch*
steps: Preheat your oven to 350F and bring a large pot of water to a boil. Peel and chop potatoes into smaller chunks, then boil until fork tender (roughly 8-10 minutes). Drain and set aside, reserving 1/2 cup of the boiling water.
Pour the reserved water into a bowl and whisk along with plant milk and olive oil.

In another large bowl, whisk together all remaining ingredients, then add the liquid ingredients to the bowl and mix. If using vegan cheese, fold it into the dough. Start with a spatula and then work the dough using your hands. It may be a little dry at first but just compact the dough and pack it tightly.

Line your baking tray with parchment paper or a nonstick baking mat and roll tablespoon sized balls of dough. Pack them tightly and space out evenly on your baking tray, leaving at least 1" between each ball of dough.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the bottom of the bread is golden brown and small cracks start to form on the surface. Enjoy!

title: Moqueca (Sem crueldade)
rawBody:
- **Source URL:** https://vegnews.com/recipes/vegan-brazilian-moqueca-hearts-of-palm-seafood-stew (Rachel Ama / VegNews; also https://thenewbaguette.com/vegetarian-moqueca/)
- **Description:** Coconut-milk-and-dendê-palm-oil stew built around hearts of palm (and optional firm tofu or banana blossom) with onion, peppers, tomato, cilantro, lime. The brininess of hearts of palm reads as seafood. Served with white rice and farofa.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $3.10 / serving · **Menu price:** $16–20
- **Weight:** 420 g (stew + rice) · **Calories:** 510 · **Protein:** 12 g · **Time:** 50 min · **Servings:** 4
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — base sauce holds; finish with lime + cilantro per ticket. Dendê oil is the signature; don't skip.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Hearts of palm + firm tofu (default) | Authentic vegan moqueca | baseline |
| Banana blossom + hearts of palm | Closer to flaky fish texture | +$0.60 |
| **Premium faux-meat:** Juicy Marbles Kinda Cod filet (sliced into stew) | "Moqueca de peixe" — premium upsell | +$4.50 → menu $22–26 |
description: Coconut-milk-and-dendê-palm-oil stew built around hearts of palm (and optional firm tofu or banana blossom) with onion, peppers, tomato, cilantro, lime. The brininess of hearts of palm reads as seafood. Served with white rice and farofa.
difficultyNote: base sauce holds; finish with lime + cilantro per ticket. Dendê oil is the signature; don't skip.

subs.from: **Premium faux-meat:** Juicy Marbles Kinda Cod filet (sliced into stew)

ingredients: 1 (14-ounce) can hearts of palm, sliced lengthways and chopped into 1-inch diagonal chunks
steps: In a large saucepan over medium heat, warm oil. Add onion and peppers and cook 5 minutes. Add paprika, chili flakes, tomato purée, and garlic and cook another 2 minutes. Stir in coconut milk, vegetable stock, hearts of palm, tomatoes, and capers. Add salt and pepper, bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and cook 30 minutes.
To pan, add half of lime juice and cook another 5 minutes. Turn off heat and stir in parsley and cilantro. Serve warm.

allergenSubs.to: oat cream + 1 tsp olive oil (per 1 cup)

title: Coxinha (Sem crueldade)
rawBody:
- **Source URL:** https://cookingwithjade.com/30-vegan-brazilian-recipes/ (Jade's roundup with vegan coxinha) — alt: NewsBytes vegan coxinha tutorial
- **Description:** Spiced jackfruit shred (or Chunk Pulled) wrapped in tapioca-or-wheat-flour dough, formed into the signature teardrop "drumstick" shape, breaded and deep-fried golden. The most beloved Brazilian street snack, veganized.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $1.20 / piece (~$3.60 / 3-piece app order) · **Menu price:** $9–12 (3-piece) / $12–15 (5-piece)
- **Weight:** 75 g / piece · **Calories:** 180 / piece · **Protein:** 5 g · **Time:** 90 min (form + chill + fry) · **Servings:** 16 pieces / batch
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Medium** — labor-heavy in shaping (par-form & freeze in service prep), then fry per ticket from frozen. Holds well frozen for 30+ days.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep (par-formed/frozen) · ⚡ Fast-Service (fry from frozen 4 min)

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Jackfruit filling (default) | Classic vegan coxinha | baseline |
| **Premium faux-meat:** Chunk Pulled filling | Heartier "chicken-style" pull, premium menu listing | +$1.10/piece |
| Beyond Chicken Pieces, shredded | Branded recognition | +$0.85/piece |
description: Spiced jackfruit shred (or Chunk Pulled) wrapped in tapioca-or-wheat-flour dough, formed into the signature teardrop "drumstick" shape, breaded and deep-fried golden. The most beloved Brazilian street snack, veganized.
difficultyNote: labor-heavy in shaping (par-form & freeze in service prep), then fry per ticket from frozen. Holds well frozen for 30+ days.
ingredients: ½ medium yellow onion, finely diced (for dough)
A few sprigs fresh parsley, chopped (plus more for filling)
1 cup (240 ml) olive oil (yes, this much — needed for dough texture)
2 to 3 cups (250–375 g) all-purpose flour, added gradually
½ red onion, finely chopped (for filling)
1 medium tomato, cored and diced (discard the wet pulp)
½ green bell pepper, finely chopped
1 cup vegan chik'n substitute (soy curls, shredded seitan, or similar), shredded
paprika, salt, pepper, garlic powder, to taste (for filling)
⅓ cup all-purpose flour (for the egg-wash slurry)
⅔ cup water (for the slurry — 1 part flour : 2 parts water)
neutral oil for deep-frying (about 1 quart / 1 L)

allergenSubs.to: gluten-free 1:1 flour blend (Bob's Red Mill or similar) + gluten-free breadcrumbs
allergenSubs.note: GF dough is more delicate — chill 20 min before shaping

allergenSubs.to: shredded young jackfruit, drained and pan-crisped
allergenSubs.note: jackfruit gives the same shredded texture; season more aggressively as it's blander

steps: Make the dough base: finely chop the half yellow onion and add it to a heavy pot with the olive oil. Sweat over medium-low heat until softened and fragrant.
Add the minced garlic to the onion-oil mixture and cook until lightly browned, about 1 minute.
In a second pot, bring the 2 cups water to a near-boil; add the onion-oil mixture just before it comes to a full boil.
Once the water boils, stir in the tomato sauce, parsley, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Reserve ¼ cup of this onion-tomato liquid for the filling.
Slowly add the flour to the boiling pot, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, until a soft dough comes together. Once cool enough to handle, knead by hand until smooth.
Roll the dough into 2-to-3-inch balls (about 10) and set aside, covered with a damp towel.
Make the filling: sauté the red onion, green pepper, and diced tomato in a hot pan, then add the shredded chik'n substitute and season with paprika, salt, pepper, and garlic powder to taste.
Add the reserved ¼ cup onion-tomato liquid a splash at a time — just enough to bind, not enough to make the filling wet (wet filling sogs the inside while frying).
Cool the filling. Flatten each dough ball into a disc, place a spoon of filling in the centre, and pinch the edges up to form the classic teardrop shape.
Whisk the flour and water (1:2) into a smooth slurry — this is the vegan "egg" wash.
Dip each coxinha in the slurry, then roll in breadcrumbs to coat fully.
Heat the neutral oil to 350°F / 175°C and deep-fry the coxinhas, a few at a time, until golden and crisp (about 3–4 minutes). Drain on paper towels.
Let cool for 2–3 minutes before serving — the filling stays scorching for longer than you'd think.

title: Churrasco / Linguiça Platter (Sem crueldade)
rawBody:
- **Source URL:** https://www.terra.com.br/vida-e-estilo/degusta/receitas/6-receitas-de-acompanhamentos-veganos-para-churrasco (technique base) + Beyond Sausage application
- **Description:** Charcoal/flame-grilled vegan linguiça (Beyond Sausage Hot Italian or Field Roast Mexican Chipotle as the closest US foodservice linguiça-calabresa profile), Beyond Steak Tips espetinhos, grilled queijo coalho-style halloumi alternative (vegan), and vegetable skewers — served with farofa de banana, salsa vinagrete, chimichurri, and pão de queijo. The vegan churrasco platter.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $7.40 / serving (sausage + tips + sides) · **Menu price:** **$24–29** (upscale) or **$32–38** (rodízio-style all-you-can-eat)
- **Weight:** 460 g (proteins + sides) · **Calories:** 720 · **Protein:** 38 g · **Time:** 30 min cook + sides bulk-preps · **Servings:** 1
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Hard** — multi-protein flame-grill plus four side components; a true churrascaria-format plate.
- **Tags:** 🥘 (sides bulk-prep) · — (proteins per ticket)

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Beyond Sausage Hot Italian + Beyond Steak Tips (default) | Best foodservice linguiça/picanha-tip stand-in available in Sysco channel | baseline |
| **Premium upgrade:** Add Juicy Marbles Loin slice as picanha course | Full rodízio experience | +$8.50 → menu $36–42 |
| Field Roast Mexican Chipotle sausage | Authentic-leaning calabresa profile | −$0.40 |
description: Charcoal/flame-grilled vegan linguiça (Beyond Sausage Hot Italian or Field Roast Mexican Chipotle as the closest US foodservice linguiça-calabresa profile), Beyond Steak Tips espetinhos, grilled queijo coalho-style halloumi alternative (vegan), and vegetable skewers — served with farofa de banana, salsa vinagrete, chimichurri, and pão de queijo. The vegan churrasco platter.
difficultyNote: multi-protein flame-grill plus four side components; a true churrascaria-format plate.

subs.from: Beyond Sausage Hot Italian + Beyond Steak Tips (default)
subs.effect: Best foodservice linguiça/picanha-tip stand-in available in Sysco channel

subs.from: **Premium upgrade:** Add Juicy Marbles Loin slice as picanha course

ingredients: 1 lb (450 g) vegan linguiça sausages (or seitan/spiced chickpea sausages)
1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
4 medium sweet plantains, peeled and halved lengthwise
fresh chimichurri (parsley, garlic, red pepper, olive oil, lime)
salt and black pepper to taste

allergenSubs.to: king trumpet mushrooms, scored and seared

title: Brigadeiro (Sem crueldade)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.piesandtacos.com/vegan-brigadeiros-vegan-truffles/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Pies and Tacos by Camila Hurst — a Brazilian baker, so this is an authoritative source. Note: Camila's recipe is a paleo / refined-sugar-free variant that uses **maple syrup + coconut butter + nut butter + vegan chocolate chips** rather than the traditional Brazilian approach with sweetened condensed milk. This is a meaningful flavor and ingredient deviation from "classic" brigadeiro — the texture is more of a dense fudge truffle than the chewy soft-roll of the original — but the result is genuinely vegan, gluten-free, and refined-sugar-free, which positions well for an upscale Brazilian menu.)*

  **Recipe** (10 pieces / batch, per Camila Hurst): 1/4 cup maple syrup, 1/4 cup coconut butter (NOT coconut oil — she's specific), 2 tbsp cashew butter (or other neutral nut butter), 1/4 cup vegan chocolate chips. Combine all ingredients in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring constantly. Heat until everything is melted and the mixture is smooth — *do not let it come to a full boil*. Pour into a heatproof bowl, cool to room temperature, then chill in the fridge 10–20 minutes until rollable consistency (not too stiff, not too soft — texture varies with kitchen temp). Scoop teaspoon-sized portions, roll into balls, coat in dairy-free sprinkles / chopped nuts / cacao nibs / shredded coconut. Refrigerated, holds up to 8 days; freezes up to 1 month.

- **Description:** Paleo-style chocolate fudge truffles built on maple syrup, coconut butter, cashew butter, and vegan chocolate chips, rolled and coated in dairy-free sprinkles. The classic Brazilian birthday-party sweet, reimagined refined-sugar-free and gluten-free. Plates as a 3- or 5-piece bite-sized dessert; the *de rigueur* finish at any Brazilian restaurant.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $0.45 / piece (~$1.35 / 3-piece — slightly higher than the traditional condensed-coconut-milk version because coconut butter and cashew butter are pricier than condensed milk, but partially offset by the small batch size) · **Menu price:** **$5–7** (3-piece) / **$7–9** (5-piece) / **$9–12** (mixed brigadeiro flight: chocolate + branco + churros-cinnamon)
- **Weight:** 25 g / piece · **Calories:** 110 · **Protein:** 1 g · **Time:** 5 min cook + 15–30 min cool + 10 min roll · **Servings:** 10 pieces / batch
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — bulk-batch, roll & coat during prep, refrigerate up to 8 days, plate cold.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Pies and Tacos paleo brigadeiro (Camila Hurst, default) | Paleo / GF / refined-sugar-free; verified vegan | baseline ($0.45) |
| Traditional vegan brigadeiro: sweetened condensed coconut milk (Nature's Charm) + cocoa + vegan butter | Closer to authentic Brazilian texture (chewy, sticky); cheaper at scale | −$0.10 → $0.35 / pc |
| Brigadeiro branco (white coconut, no chocolate) | Pair-tier alongside chocolate | flat |
| Beijinho (white coconut + clove + sprinkles) | Cousin sweet | flat |
| Mixed-brigadeiro flight of 3 flavors | Premium menu listing | +$1.20 → menu $9–12 |
urlNote: Pies and Tacos by Camila Hurst — a Brazilian baker, so this is an authoritative source. Note: Camila's recipe is a paleo / refined-sugar-free variant that uses **maple syrup + coconut butter + nut butter + vegan chocolate chips** rather than the traditional Brazilian approach with sweetened condensed milk. This is a meaningful flavor and ingredient deviation from "classic" brigadeiro — the texture is more of a dense fudge truffle than the chewy soft-roll of the original — but the result is genuinely vegan, gluten-free, and refined-sugar-free, which positions well for an upscale Brazilian menu.
description: Paleo-style chocolate fudge truffles built on maple syrup, coconut butter, cashew butter, and vegan chocolate chips, rolled and coated in dairy-free sprinkles. The classic Brazilian birthday-party sweet, reimagined refined-sugar-free and gluten-free. Plates as a 3- or 5-piece bite-sized dessert; the *de rigueur* finish at any Brazilian restaurant.
difficultyNote: bulk-batch, roll & coat during prep, refrigerate up to 8 days, plate cold.

subs.from: Pies and Tacos paleo brigadeiro (Camila Hurst, default)
Traditional vegan brigadeiro: sweetened condensed coconut milk (Nature's Charm) + cocoa + vegan butter
subs.effect: Closer to authentic Brazilian texture (chewy, sticky); cheaper at scale

ingredients: ½ cup (50 g) chocolate sprinkles (or cocoa powder, for coating)
1 tablespoon coconut oil (to grease hands for rolling)
steps: Mix all ingredients in a small saucepan, over medium heat. Stir constantly while the mixture heats up. You are going to bring it to almost a boil, but not quite.
As soon as all ingredients are melted, and mixture is smooth, remove from the heat and pour in a heat proof bowl.
Let mixture rest at room temperature until completely cooled down.
Put it in the fridge for about 10-20 minutes.
Use a tablespoon to scoop balls and roll them in sprinkles, chopped nuts, seeds, shredded coconut, cacao nibs, or any other topping you can think of.

This process of letting it come to room temperature and then chilling the brigadeiro for 10-20 minutes will ensure the optimal texture for rolling. If your brigadeiro is too cold, it will be too stiff to be rolled. If it’s too warm, it will be too soft. You need to get it to be just the right consistency, so you may have to chill it a bit longer, depending on how hot your kitchen is.
These will store really well in the fridge for up to 8 days.
You can freeze them for up to 1 month, very well packaged, let it thaw in the fridge.

allergenSubs.to: oat cream + 1 tsp olive oil (per 1 cup)
